symptom: Deep clean a toaster without damaging heating elements (NYT Wirecutter method)
tools_required: Bar Keepers Friend (optional, for stainless steel exterior only)

preconditions: Do not turn upside down and shake vigorously — can damage delicate heating elements
hazards: Water in slots, levers, or buttons can cause electric shock or short if plugged in wet
Never stick sharp objects into the toaster

steps.action: Unplug the toaster and let it cool completely
steps.what_to_look_for: No power to unit; cool exterior

steps.action: Use pastry brush and long thin paintbrush to gently brush crumbs from heating elements toward the bottom — do not shake the toaster upside down as primary method
steps.what_to_look_for: Crumbs dislodged with soft bristles only; no metal tools
steps.visual_confirmation: Visible crumb reduction in slot area
steps.safety_note: Wirecutter: avoid shaking as main method — elements are delicate

steps.action: Remove crumb tray and empty into trash; wipe tray with damp paper towel or cloth; dry fully before reinserting
steps.visual_confirmation: Tray slides back in without moisture

steps.action: Wipe outside with damp towel; for stubborn stains use a little dish soap on sponge — keep water out of slots, levers, and buttons
steps.visual_confirmation: Exterior clean; no water pooled near controls

steps.action: Dry the toaster thoroughly with a towel
steps.what_to_look_for: All surfaces dry, especially around slots
steps.visual_confirmation: No dampness before storage or next use

steps.action: If exterior is stainless steel, polish with Bar Keepers Friend per product instructions (exterior only)
steps.what_to_look_for: Stainless finish; test small area first
steps.visual_confirmation: Even polish without residue entering slots
steps.if_issue_found: Skip if not stainless or if user unsure — cosmetic only

sources: NYT Wirecutter — How to Clean A Toaster (YouTube lpNJUgBSvn0)
